title: Motion and Animation
summary: Using purposeful motion to signal state, shift focus, and add delight, like Gemini's breathing response motion.
body:
## First principles
Motion in UI isn't just decoration; it's communication. It explains changes in state, establishes hierarchy, and masks loading times. In AI interfaces, where states (thinking, streaming, idle) are fluid, motion provides critical context.

## Purposeful motion
Every animation should serve a purpose. When an AI is "thinking," a subtle, organic pulsing or "breathing" animation signals life and activity without being distracting. When a response finishes, easing the layout signals completion.

## Material design principles
Follow established physics: use ease-in-out for natural movement. Avoid linear animations which feel robotic. The choreography of elements should guide the user's eye from the prompt to the emerging response.
analogy: Motion in UI is like body language in a conversation. A nod, a thinking expression, or a gesture tells you what the other person is doing before they even speak.
diagram:
flowchart TD
  Idle[Idle: Static] --> |Input| Think[Thinking: Organic Pulse]
  Think --> |Generate| Stream[Streaming: Smooth Reveal]
  Stream --> |Done| Settle[Settle: Ease to Rest]

examples.what: Uses a glowing, breathing star animation to indicate thinking, giving the AI a subtle, organic presence.
Uses a glowing edge animation around the screen to indicate the assistant is listening and processing.

drills.a: It feels robotic and unnatural compared to ease-in-out which mimics real-world physics.
Animation that communicates state, shifts focus, or masks latency, rather than just being decorative.

teachItBack: Motion acts as the body language of your UI. When the AI is thinking, a subtle breathing animation tells the user the system is alive and working. Good motion guides the user's eye and uses natural easing to feel fluid, not robotic.

glossary.definition: The rate of change of a parameter over time, used to make animations feel natural (e.g., ease-in-out).

quiz.q: What is the primary role of a 'breathing' animation during AI generation?
choices: To signal that the system is active and processing
To distract the user from reading
quiz.explanation: An organic, pulsing animation reassures the user that the system hasn't frozen and is actively working on their request.
